commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The remaining action is protected maintainer review of compatibility and availability risk, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression found; the diff does not change dependencies, workflows, lockfiles, package resolution, secrets handling, or downloaded code execution. (sha=8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR converts shell completion, startup channel maintenance, browser residue repair, and systemd linger doctor checks into structured detect/repair health checks while preserving their doctor contribution positions.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a PR migration of doctor maintenance behavior rather than a bug report. The submitted proof does show the after-fix shell-completion and systemd-linger CLI paths on the latest head.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is protected maintainer review of compatibility and availability risk, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Converts the next interactive doctor maintenance bucket to the structured `detect()` / `repair()` health contract introduced by #83198 while preserving the original doctor contribution order. The explicit goal is isomorphism: each converted legacy `run(ctx)` repair should keep its old sequencing and side effects while gaining a provable `detect -> repair -> detect` validation path.

This slice covers:

- shell completion detection and cached-completion repair
- startup channel maintenance as a repair-only structured health check
- Linux systemd linger detection and guided repair for local Gateway services

The important follow-up in this revision is that converted repairs no longer run from a separate early `doctor:structured-health-repairs` contribution. Each converted contribution now invokes its mapped health check from its existing slot in the doctor order, so the contract changes without changing sequencing. This also realigns the first merged health-check slice from #80055: detect-only checks keep their existing contribution positions, skills repair remains ordered at `doctor:skills`, and the browser residue repair added by #83230 runs from `doctor:browser`. Existing conversion metadata still advertises `healthCheckIds` for lint filtering and conversion tracking.

## Behavior

- `doctor --lint` can report shell-completion and systemd-linger findings without mutating state.
- `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion` stays quiet when shell completion is simply not installed, because completion install is optional.
- `doctor --fix` runs converted `repair()` paths at the same point where the legacy contribution previously ran, then validates with the same scoped `detect()` logic.
- Startup channel maintenance remains repair-only: it is intentionally quiet in lint mode, but participates in the structured repair pipeline during `doctor --fix` at the startup-channel contribution position.
- Browser clawd-profile residue repair from the earlier conversion path is also kept at the browser contribution position rather than an early bulk pass.
- Dry-run/diff readiness is covered now for the eventual doctor preview payoff, but the user-facing doctor dry-run/diff flag should stay off until the end of the migration after all repair items either support preview metadata or are explicitly classified. In this PR, `diff: false` still runs or previews repair without structured diff payloads, while side-effect repairs report `effects[]` and intentionally empty `diffs[]` because there is no config/file patch to render.

## Follow-up Landscape

This PR should be treated as the interactive-maintenance repair slice, not the final dry-run/diff feature switch. The remaining branches should continue the same migration pattern:

- convert the next legacy repair bucket from procedural `run(ctx)` into `detect()` / `repair()` while preserving contribution order;
- add `detect -> repair -> detect` proof for each repair that claims ownership;
- classify dry-run behavior for every repair as either config/file `diffs[]`, side-effect `effects[]`, or explicitly non-previewable until later;
- keep the public doctor dry-run/diff CLI disabled until all repair buckets have been migrated or classified, so preview output is honest across the whole command.

The expected end state is that `doctor --fix` remains the mutating path, while the eventual dry-run/diff mode can reuse the same repair contract to show planned config/file patches and side effects without applying them.
Proof policy for this migration series: every repair conversion should carry its own evidence as it lands. For each branch, the PR should show the old-to-new repair path with either real CLI output from disposable state or an explicit maintainer-accepted reason that the path is injection-only. That keeps the final dry-run/diff rollout honest: each repair item is proven before the public preview flag is enabled, instead of waiting for a broad end-of-series audit.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Azure CLI Proof For Converted Repairs

Fresh current-head proof for `8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb` was run on a disposable Azure Ubuntu 24.04 VM with a real user systemd manager and real `loginctl` linger state.

Artifacts:

- Full Azure CLI transcript: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/azure-doctor-cli-proof.redacted.log
- Azure proof excerpt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/azure-proof-excerpt.txt
- Azure proof summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/summary.redacted.txt
- Artifact README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/README.md
- Docker repro for the non-privileged shell-completion CLI path: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/docker-repro.sh

Key observed results from the Azure transcript:

```text
shell completion lint before fix exit=1 expected=1
shell completion doctor fix exit=0 expected=0
shell completion lint after fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd user manager status exit=0 expected=0
systemd disposable unit enabled exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger before fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint before fix exit=1 expected=1
systemd linger doctor fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger after fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint after fix exit=0 expected=0
```

The transcript shows:

- `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion --json` detected slow dynamic completion and exited 1 before repair.
- `doctor --fix --yes` rewrote the disposable bash profile to cached completion.
- the same shell-completion lint command returned `{"ok":true,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":19,"findings":[]}` after repair.
- `loginctl show-user azureuser -p Linger` returned `Linger=no` before repair.
- `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/systemd-linger --json` reported the systemd linger warning and exited 1 before repair.
- `doctor --fix --yes` printed `Enabled systemd lingering for azureuser.` while running against a disposable enabled user systemd unit.
- `loginctl show-user azureuser -p Linger` returned `Linger=yes` after repair.
- the same systemd-linger lint command returned `{"ok":true,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":19,"findings":[]}` after repair.

The Docker repro artifact is included because maintainers asked for Docker repros for tricky issues. It covers the repeatable non-privileged shell-completion CLI path. The privileged systemd/loginctl behavior is intentionally proven on the disposable Azure VM instead of Docker, because containerized systemd/loginctl proof requires privileged host integration and is less representative than a real Ubuntu VM.

### Focused Test/Type Proof
![PR 83753 terminal proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/pr-83753-real-proof-terminal.png)

Real proof artifacts for the latest pushed head `8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb`:

- Full command transcript: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof.clean.log
- Output excerpt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof-excerpt.txt
- Artifact README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/README.md

The proof reran the focused tests/checks after the latest push and captured the actual stdout/stderr plus per-command exit codes. The split Vitest runs cover the converted repair surfaces and the backfilled #80055/#83198 dry-run/diff API paths.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node node_modules\oxfmt\bin\oxfmt --check --threads=1 src\commands\doctor-completion.test.ts src\commands\doctor.types.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.test.ts src\flows\doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src\flows\doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src\flows\doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts
node node_modules\@typescript\native-preview\bin\tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false
```

Observed proof from the transcript:

```text
doctor completion + systemd linger tests exit=0
startup channel + browser residue tests exit=0
core checks tests exit=0
health contributions tests exit=0
repair flow dry-run diff tests exit=0
changed file format check exit=0
core test tsgo exit=0
```

The Vitest output shows 7 focused files and 58 tests passed across the split runs:

```text
src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts + src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts: 2 files / 10 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts + src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts: 2 files / 6 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts: 1 file / 8 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts: 1 file / 28 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts: 1 file / 6 tests passed
```

- **Behavior addressed**: Follow-up doctor maintenance checks migrate from legacy `run(ctx)` repair logic into structured health checks without changing the user-facing repair order; optional shell completion installs do not become lint failures.
- **Real environment tested**: Windows source checkout at `doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance` head `8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb`, using repo `node` wrappers rather than raw Vitest.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: The commands listed above were run after the latest push; the raw transcript linked above includes the command lines, test names, pass summaries, and exit codes.
- **Evidence after fix**: The converted shell completion, startup channel maintenance, browser residue, systemd linger, contribution-order, and repair-flow dry-run/diff tests all passed. Changed-file formatting passed. The core test TypeScript config exited 0.
- **Observed result after fix**: Each converted repair path now has focused proof for `detect -> repair -> detect` or repair-position behavior, while dry-run/diff backfill proves preview metadata is threaded without enabling the public CLI mode yet.
- **What was not tested**: No live systemd service was modified. The systemd linger repair path is covered with injected `loginctl` dependencies rather than changing the host login manager. I did not run a live shell-profile `doctor --fix` install because that would modify the proof host profile; the cache/install repair behavior is covered by injected repair tests.

## Verification

- Real command transcript artifact: `proof.clean.log` linked above.
- Focused Vitest proof: 7 files / 58 tests passed across split runs.
- Changed-file formatting: passed.
- Core test `tsgo`: passed.
target.author: giodl73-repo

```text
systemd user manager status exit=0 expected=0
systemd disposable unit enabled exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger before fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint before fix exit=1 expected=1
systemd linger doctor fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger after fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint after fix exit=0 expected=0
```

The transcript shows:

- `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion --json` detected slow dynamic completion and exited 1 before repair.
- `doctor --fix --yes` rewrote the disposable bash profile to cached completion.
- the same shell-completion lint command returned `{"ok":true,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":19,"findings":[]}` after repair.
- `loginctl show-user azureuser -p Linger` returned `Linger=no` before repair.
- `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/systemd-linger --json` reported the systemd linger warning and exited 1 before repair.
- `doctor --fix --yes` printed `Enabled systemd lingering for azureuser.` while running against a disposable enabled user systemd unit.
- `loginctl show-user azureuser -p Linger` returned `Linger=yes` after repair.
- the same systemd-linger lint command returned `{"ok":true,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":19,"findings":[]}` after repair.

The Docker repro artifact is included because maintainers asked for Docker repros for tricky issues. It covers the repeatable non-privileged shell-completion CLI path. The privileged systemd/loginctl behavior is intentionally proven on the disposable Azure VM instead of Docker, because containerized systemd/loginctl proof requires privileged host integration and is less representative than a real Ubuntu VM.

### Focused Test/Type Proof
![PR 83753 terminal proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/pr-83753-real-proof-terminal.png)

- Full command transcript: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof.clean.log
- Output excerpt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof-excerpt.txt
- Artifact README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/README.md

The proof reran the focused tests/checks after the latest push and captured the actual stdout/stderr plus per-command exit codes. The split Vitest runs cover the converted repair surfaces and the backfilled #80055/#83198 dry-run/diff API paths.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node node_modules\oxfmt\bin\oxfmt --check --threads=1 src\commands\doctor-completion.test.ts src\commands\doctor.types.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.test.ts src\flows\doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src\flows\doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src\flows\doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts
node node_modules\@typescript\native-preview\bin\tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false
```

Observed proof from the transcript:

```text
doctor completion + systemd linger tests exit=0
startup channel + browser residue tests exit=0
core checks tests exit=0
health contributions tests exit=0
repair flow dry-run diff tests exit=0
changed file format check exit=0
core test tsgo exit=0
```

The Vitest output shows 7 focused files and 58 tests passed across the split runs:

```text
src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts + src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts: 2 files / 10 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts + src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts: 2 files / 6 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts: 1 file / 8 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts: 1 file / 28 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts: 1 file / 6 tests passed
```

- **Behavior addressed**: Follow-up doctor maintenance checks migrate from legacy `run(ctx)` repair logic into structured health checks without changing the user-facing repair order; optional shell completion installs do not become lint failures.
- **Real environment tested**: Windows source checkout at `doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance` head `8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb`, using repo `node` wrappers rather than raw Vitest.

I did not run a live shell-profile `doctor --fix` install because that would modify the proof host profile; the cache/install repair behavior is covered by injected repair tests.

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Converts the next interactive doctor maintenance bucket to the structured `detect()` / `repair()` health contract introduced by #83198 while preserving the original doctor contribution order. The explicit goal is isomorphism: each converted legacy `run(ctx)` repair should keep its old sequencing and side effects while gaining a provable `detect -> repair -> detect` validation path.

This slice covers:

- shell completion detection and cached-completion repair
- startup channel maintenance as a repair-only structured health check
- Linux systemd linger detection and guided repair for local Gateway services

The important follow-up in this revision is that converted repairs no longer run from a separate early `doctor:structured-health-repairs` contribution. Each converted contribution now invokes its mapped health check from its existing slot in the doctor order, so the contract changes without changing sequencing. This also realigns the first merged health-check slice from #80055: detect-only checks keep their existing contribution positions, skills repair remains ordered at `doctor:skills`, and the browser residue repair added by #83230 runs from `doctor:browser`. Existing conversion metadata still advertises `healthCheckIds` for lint filtering and conversion tracking.

## Behavior

- `doctor --lint` can report shell-completion and systemd-linger findings without mutating state.
- `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion` stays quiet when shell completion is simply not installed, because completion install is optional.
- `doctor --fix` runs converted `repair()` paths at the same point where the legacy contribution previously ran, then validates with the same scoped `detect()` logic.
- Startup channel maintenance remains repair-only: it is intentionally quiet in lint mode, but participates in the structured repair pipeline during `doctor --fix` at the startup-channel contribution position.
- Browser clawd-profile residue repair from the earlier conversion path is also kept at the browser contribution position rather than an early bulk pass.
- Dry-run/diff readiness is covered now for the eventual doctor preview payoff, but the user-facing doctor dry-run/diff flag should stay off until the end of the migration after all repair items either support preview metadata or are explicitly classified. In this PR, `diff: false` still runs or previews repair without structured diff payloads, while side-effect repairs report `effects[]` and intentionally empty `diffs[]` because there is no config/file patch to render.

## Follow-up Landscape

This PR should be treated as the interactive-maintenance repair slice, not the final dry-run/diff feature switch. The remaining branches should continue the same migration pattern:

- convert the next legacy repair bucket from procedural `run(ctx)` into `detect()` / `repair()` while preserving contribution order;
- add `detect -> repair -> detect` proof for each repair that claims ownership;
- classify dry-run behavior for every repair as either config/file `diffs[]`, side-effect `effects[]`, or explicitly non-previewable until later;
- keep the public doctor dry-run/diff CLI disabled until all repair buckets have been migrated or classified, so preview output is honest across the whole command.

The expected end state is that `doctor --fix` remains the mutating path, while the eventual dry-run/diff mode can reuse the same repair contract to show planned config/file patches and side effects without applying them.
Proof policy for this migration series: every repair conversion should carry its own evidence as it lands. For each branch, the PR should show the old-to-new repair path with either real CLI output from disposable state or an explicit maintainer-accepted reason that the path is injection-only. That keeps the final dry-run/diff rollout honest: each repair item is proven before the public preview flag is enabled, instead of waiting for a broad end-of-series audit.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Azure CLI Proof For Converted Repairs

Fresh current-head proof for `8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb` was run on a disposable Azure Ubuntu 24.04 VM with a real user systemd manager and real `loginctl` linger state.

Artifacts:

- Full Azure CLI transcript: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/azure-doctor-cli-proof.redacted.log
- Azure proof excerpt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/azure-proof-excerpt.txt
- Azure proof summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/summary.redacted.txt
- Artifact README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/README.md
- Docker repro for the non-privileged shell-completion CLI path: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/docker-repro.sh

Key observed results from the Azure transcript:

```text
shell completion lint before fix exit=1 expected=1
shell completion doctor fix exit=0 expected=0
shell completion lint after fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd user manager status exit=0 expected=0
systemd disposable unit enabled exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger before fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint before fix exit=1 expected=1
systemd linger doctor fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger after fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint after fix exit=0 expected=0
```

The transcript shows:

- `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion --json` detected slow dynamic completion and exited 1 before repair.
- `doctor --fix --yes` rewrote the disposable bash profile to cached completion.
- the same shell-completion lint command returned `{"ok":true,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":19,"findings":[]}` after repair.
- `loginctl show-user azureuser -p Linger` returned `Linger=no` before repair.
- `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/systemd-linger --json` reported the systemd linger warning and exited 1 before repair.
- `doctor --fix --yes` printed `Enabled systemd lingering for azureuser.` while running against a disposable enabled user systemd unit.
- `loginctl show-user azureuser -p Linger` returned `Linger=yes` after repair.
- the same systemd-linger lint command returned `{"ok":true,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":19,"findings":[]}` after repair.

The Docker repro artifact is included because maintainers asked for Docker repros for tricky issues. It covers the repeatable non-privileged shell-completion CLI path. The privileged systemd/loginctl behavior is intentionally proven on the disposable Azure VM instead of Docker, because containerized systemd/loginctl proof requires privileged host integration and is less representative than a real Ubuntu VM.

### Focused Test/Type Proof
![PR 83753 terminal proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/pr-83753-real-proof-terminal.png)

Real proof artifacts for the latest pushed head `8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb`:

- Full command transcript: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof.clean.log
- Output excerpt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof-excerpt.txt
- Artifact README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/README.md

The proof reran the focused tests/checks after the latest push and captured the actual stdout/stderr plus per-command exit codes. The split Vitest runs cover the converted repair surfaces and the backfilled #80055/#83198 dry-run/diff API paths.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node node_modules\oxfmt\bin\oxfmt --check --threads=1 src\commands\doctor-completion.test.ts src\commands\doctor.types.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.test.ts src\flows\doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src\flows\doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src\flows\doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts
node node_modules\@typescript\native-preview\bin\tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false
```

Observed proof from the transcript:

```text
doctor completion + systemd linger tests exit=0
startup channel + browser residue tests exit=0
core checks tests exit=0
health contributions tests exit=0
repair flow dry-run diff tests exit=0
changed file format check exit=0
core test tsgo exit=0
```

The Vitest output shows 7 focused files and 58 tests passed across the split runs:

```text
src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts + src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts: 2 files / 10 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts + src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts: 2 files / 6 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts: 1 file / 8 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts: 1 file / 28 tests passed
src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts: 1 file / 6 tests passed
```

- **Behavior addressed**: Follow-up doctor maintenance checks migrate from legacy `run(ctx)` repair logic into structured health checks without changing the user-facing repair order; optional shell completion installs do not become lint failures.
- **Real environment tested**: Windows source checkout at `doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance` head `8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb`, using repo `node` wrappers rather than raw Vitest.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: The commands listed above were run after the latest push; the raw transcript linked above includes the command lines, test names, pass summaries, and exit codes.
- **Evidence after fix**: The converted shell completion, startup channel maintenance, browser residue, systemd linger, contribution-order, and repair-flow dry-run/diff tests all passed. Changed-file formatting passed. The core test TypeScript config exited 0.
- **Observed result after fix**: Each converted repair path now has focused proof for `detect -> repair -> detect` or repair-position behavior, while dry-run/diff backfill proves preview metadata is threaded without enabling the public CLI mode yet.
- **What was not tested**: No live systemd service was modified. The systemd linger repair path is covered with injected `loginctl` dependencies rather than changing the host login manager. I did not run a live shell-profile `doctor --fix` install because that would modify the proof host profile; the cache/install repair behavior is covered by injected repair tests.

## Verification

- Real command transcript artifact: `proof.clean.log` linked above.
- Focused Vitest proof: 7 files / 58 tests passed across split runs.
- Changed-file formatting: passed.
- Core test `tsgo`: passed.
